recent_coach_change: Major recent saga: Advocaat guided Curacao through qualification, then stepped down early 2026 over his daughter's health. Fred Rutten took over but parted ways after two heavy March friendly defeats and player-council pressure; Advocaat was reappointed in May 2026 to lead the team at the finals, becoming the oldest head coach (78) ever at a World Cup.
qualification_path: First-ever World Cup qualification via CONCACAF: won all four matches of their initial group, then topped the final group (above Jamaica and Trinidad & Tobago) to claim a direct berth
key_players: Leandro Bacuna (captain) — most-capped player (71), midfield leader with European top-division experience and set-piece quality
Eloy Room — veteran goalkeeper, joint most-capped (71)
Rangelo Janga (FC Eindhoven) — all-time top scorer (21 goals in 43 caps)
Juninho Bacuna — central midfielder, 13 international goals, dangerous through qualifying
Squad bolstered by Dutch-developed, Curacaoan-heritage players under the FIFA eligibility rule
squad_notes: Curacao will be the smallest country (population ~150,000) ever to play at a World Cup and one of the most inexperienced squads, leaning heavily on Dutch-developed players of Curacaoan descent eligible via the FIFA rule on youth-level representation.
warmup_assessment: Poor and mixed: heavy March defeats (5-1 to Australia, 2-0 to China) helped trigger the coaching change, then a 4-1 loss to Scotland on 30 May; only a 4-0 win over minnows Aruba (6 June) offered late respite.
shape_verdict: A historic, feel-good debutant but in shaky form after chastening warm-up defeats and a destabilising coaching merry-go-round; Advocaat's return brings calm but expectations are very low in a brutal group.

news.headline: Curacao reinstate Dick Advocaat; he will become oldest World Cup coach after Rutten exit
Scotland beat Curacao 4-1 in pre-World Cup friendly at Hampden
Curacao beat Aruba 4-0 in final World Cup warm-up
